title: koeleria macrantha subsp. glauca
intro: Koeleria macrantha subsp. glauca, also known as blue hair grass, is a subspecies of Koeleria macrantha, a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Koeleria macrantha subsp. glauca include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
